report.name: Initial Waste Assessment - Small Manufacturing Facility

categories.name: Production

attachments.name: waste_walk_production_chaos.jpg
attachments.description: Initial waste walk findings showing significant waste in all categories throughout production

categories.description: Production operations reveal extensive waste in all categories with no systematic approach to identification or elimination.

statements.name: Excessive waiting waste throughout production process
statements.value: Workers idle 40% of time waiting for materials, instructions, or equipment

statements.name: Severe motion waste with disorganized workstations
statements.value: Tools scattered, supplies distant, workers constantly searching and reaching

statements.name: No pull system resulting in massive overproduction
statements.value: Making to forecast with 3+ months of finished goods inventory accumulated

statements.name: No standard work leading to process variations
statements.value: Each operator using different methods with varying cycle times and quality

categories.name: Quality
categories.description: Quality management essentially non-existent with high defect rates and no systematic improvement approach.

statements.name: Defect rates exceeding 8% with customer complaints
statements.value: No incoming inspection, in-process checks, or final verification performed

statements.name: Large rework area accepting defects as normal
statements.value: 25% of workforce dedicated to fixing problems rather than prevention

statements.name: No error-proofing or mistake prevention methods
statements.value: Relying entirely on operator attention with no aids or devices

statements.name: Employee skills wasted with no input mechanism
statements.value: Workers discouraged from suggesting improvements after ideas ignored

categories.name: Maintenance

attachments.name: waste_walk_equipment_neglect.jpg
attachments.description: Equipment maintenance neglect contributing to multiple waste types

categories.description: No maintenance program exists with equipment running to failure causing extensive operational waste.

statements.name: Equipment failures cause 4+ hours daily production loss
statements.value: No preventive maintenance with machines breaking down unpredictably

statements.name: Maintenance tools and parts completely disorganized
statements.value: Hours spent searching for tools and waiting for parts orders

statements.name: Equipment severely overburdened beyond capacity
statements.value: Running at 150% rated speed to meet demand causing premature failures

statements.name: Environmental waste from leaking and inefficient equipment
statements.value: Oil leaks, compressed air losses, and energy waste throughout facility

categories.name: Administration
categories.description: Administrative processes completely paper-based with extreme overprocessing and information waste.

statements.name: Overwhelming paper inventory with lost documents
statements.value: Rooms full of paper files with frequent inability to find needed information

statements.name: Extreme overprocessing with quadruple data entry
statements.value: Same data handwritten on multiple forms then entered in different systems

statements.name: Uncontrolled social media use affecting productivity
statements.value: Employees on phones constantly with no policy or awareness of impact

statements.name: Severe workload unevenness causing stress and errors
statements.value: Panic at month-end with 80-hour weeks while mid-month sees make-work

highlightsData.title: Pervasive Waiting Waste
highlightsData.description:
Production Standstills: Workers idle 40% of shift waiting for materials from chaotic warehouse, unclear instructions from supervisors, or broken equipment repairs.

Information Bottlenecks: Critical production information stuck in office paperwork with 2-3 hour delays for basic decisions and approvals.

highlightsData.title: Extreme Motion Inefficiency
highlightsData.description:
Workstation Chaos: No 5S implementation with tools scattered across facility, requiring constant searching and walking to gather materials for each job.

Layout Dysfunction: Related processes separated by entire building length with workers making 50+ trips daily between stations.

highlightsData.title: Transport Chaos
highlightsData.description:
Material Handling Waste: Products moved 7-8 times between processes due to no flow planning, with forklifts constantly crisscrossing paths.

Storage Randomness: Materials stored wherever space available requiring extensive searching and transport to locate needed items.

highlightsData.title: Rampant Overprocessing
highlightsData.description:
Unnecessary Operations: Multiple inspection steps adding no value, excessive packaging beyond customer requirements, and gold-plating of basic products.

Information Redundancy: Same production data recorded on 4 different forms, entered in 3 systems, with weekly reports no one reads.

highlightsData.title: Inventory Explosion
highlightsData.description:
Overproduction Results: Three months of finished goods inventory cramming warehouse, with oldest items becoming obsolete before shipping.

Raw Material Excess: Six months of raw materials purchased in bulk for discounts now deteriorating and consuming valuable space.

highlightsData.title: Defect Epidemic
highlightsData.description:
Quality Crisis: 8% defect rate with 25% of workforce dedicated to rework, customer complaints increasing, and no root cause analysis performed.

Hidden Defects: Problems discovered by customers rather than internal checks, damaging reputation and requiring expensive returns processing.

highlightsData.title: Skills Completely Wasted
highlightsData.description:
Demoralized Workforce: Employees report having given up on improvements after years of ignored suggestions, now just doing minimum required.

No Development: Zero training programs with employees learning through trial and error, perpetuating bad practices and inefficiencies.

highlightsData.title: Massive Overproduction
highlightsData.description:
Forecast-Driven Chaos: Production based on optimistic sales forecasts rather than actual demand, creating mountains of unsold inventory.

Batch Mentality: Large batch sizes to maximize equipment utilization creating excess inventory, quality problems, and cash flow crisis.

actionItems.title: Conduct Basic 5S Implementation
actionItems.description: Start fundamental workplace organization in one pilot area to demonstrate waste reduction potential

actionItems.title: Establish Basic Visual Management
actionItems.description: Create simple production boards to make waiting waste visible and begin addressing root causes

attachments.name: waste_walk_action_plan.jpg
attachments.description: Initial action plan to address critical waste findings

actionItems.title: Train Leadership in Waste Identification
actionItems.description: Provide basic lean training to management team focusing on 8 wastes identification
